title: Descaling Required
shortDescription: The machine needs descaling to remove mineral buildup from the internal heating element and water lines.
fullDescription: The DESCALE indicator activates after a set number of brew cycles to remind you to descale. Mineral deposits from water accumulate inside the heating element and water lines, reducing brewing performance and temperature. Hard water areas require more frequent descaling.

causes.cause: Normal mineral buildup from regular use
Hard water accelerating scale buildup
Previous descaling not completed properly
Internal counter reached descale threshold

symptoms: DESCALE message on display or dedicated LED illuminated
Coffee brewing slower than usual
Brew temperature lower than normal
Incomplete cups or short pours
Machine makes more noise during brewing

fixSteps.title: Prepare the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the water reservoir. Remove any water filter cartridge. Do not insert a K-Cup pod.
tools: Keurig descaling solution or white vinegar

fixSteps.title: Fill with descaling solution
fixSteps.instruction: Pour the entire bottle of Keurig descaling solution into the reservoir, then add fresh water to fill to the MAX line.

fixSteps.title: Run descaling brew cycles
fixSteps.instruction: Place a large mug on the drip tray. Select the largest cup size and brew. Repeat until the ADD WATER indicator turns on, discarding the liquid each time.

fixSteps.title: Rinse cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Fill the reservoir with fresh water and run at least 12 large cup rinse brews to flush all descaling solution from the system. The water should have no taste or smell.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Descaling is always a DIY task.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig descale light how to descale fix

faq.q: How often should I descale my Keurig?
faq.a: Every 3-6 months, or when the DESCALE indicator activates. More often with hard water.

faq.q: Can I use vinegar instead of Keurig solution?
faq.a: White vinegar works but requires more rinse cycles (12+) to remove the taste. Keurig solution rinses more easily.

faq.q: Why won't the descale light turn off?
faq.a: You must complete the full descaling procedure including all rinse cycles. On some models, hold the 8oz and 12oz buttons simultaneously for 3 seconds to reset.

title: Machine Needs Priming — Air Lock
shortDescription: The water system has an air lock and needs to be primed to restore water flow.
fullDescription: The PRIME error occurs when the machine cannot draw water from the reservoir. This usually happens after the reservoir runs empty during brewing, after descaling, when the machine has been stored without use, or after moving the machine.

causes.cause: Air lock in the water line after running dry
Water reservoir not properly seated on intake valve
Debris blocking the water inlet needle or valve
Pump unable to generate suction due to wear

symptoms: PRIME message on display
Machine attempts to brew but no water comes out
Pump sounds louder than normal or strains
Machine stops mid-brew without dispensing
Gurgling or sputtering noises

fixSteps.title: Reseat the water reservoir
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water reservoir, empty it, and clean the connection area. Refill with fresh water and firmly reinsert until it clicks or seats properly.

fixSteps.title: Tap and shake gently
fixSteps.instruction: With the reservoir installed, gently tap the bottom of the machine to dislodge air bubbles near the intake valve.

fixSteps.title: Run water-only brew cycles
fixSteps.instruction: Without a K-Cup, run 3-5 water-only brew cycles on the largest cup size to push air through the system and establish water flow.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet needle
fixSteps.instruction: Check the water reservoir inlet for mineral buildup or debris. Clean with a paperclip or the Keurig needle maintenance tool.

whenToCallPro: If the pump has failed and cannot generate suction after all cleaning and priming steps.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig prime error fix air lock reservoir

faq.q: What causes a Keurig to need priming?
faq.a: Running the reservoir dry introduces air into the water line. Always refill before it empties completely.

faq.q: Why does my Keurig say PRIME with a full reservoir?
faq.a: The reservoir may not be properly seated, or air bubbles are trapped near the intake. Remove, refill, and reseat firmly.

faq.q: Can a clogged needle cause PRIME?
faq.a: Yes, if the water inlet is partially blocked, the pump cannot draw water, triggering the PRIME error.

title: Water Reservoir Empty or Not Detected
shortDescription: The machine cannot detect sufficient water in the reservoir.
fullDescription: The ADD WATER indicator means the water level is below the minimum required for brewing, the reservoir is not properly installed, or the water level sensor float is stuck.

causes.cause: Water reservoir is empty or below minimum level
Reservoir not properly seated on the machine
Magnetic float valve in reservoir stuck
Water level sensor malfunction

symptoms: ADD WATER message or indicator light active
Machine refuses to start a brew cycle
Reservoir appears to have water but is not detected
Error persists after refilling the reservoir

fixSteps.title: Fill the reservoir
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the reservoir, fill to the MAX line with fresh water, and reinsert firmly until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Check the float valve
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the reservoir for the magnetic float. Shake gently to ensure it moves freely and is not stuck in the down position.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the bottom of the reservoir and the sensor contact area on the machine with a damp cloth to remove mineral deposits.

fixSteps.title: Try without a water filter
fixSteps.instruction: If using a water filter, remove it temporarily to test if the filter is interfering with the float mechanism.

whenToCallPro: If the water level sensor has failed and the float moves freely.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig add water error full reservoir fix

faq.q: Why does my Keurig say ADD WATER when it is full?
faq.a: The magnetic float may be stuck. Remove the reservoir, shake it gently, and reinsert.

faq.q: Can I use my Keurig without the water filter?
faq.a: Yes, the water filter is optional. It improves taste but is not required for operation.

faq.q: Why does the K-Mini show ADD WATER?
faq.a: The K-Mini has a small reservoir that must be filled for each cup. Fill to the desired cup size line.

title: Machine Will Not Start Brewing
shortDescription: The machine powers on but will not start a brew cycle when the button is pressed.
fullDescription: The machine appears functional (lights on, display working) but pressing the brew button does not initiate a brew cycle. This can be caused by various issues from a clogged needle to a lid that is not fully closed.

causes.cause: K-Cup handle or lid not fully closed and latched
Entrance or exit needles clogged with coffee grounds
No K-Cup pod detected by the machine
Internal water line clogged or air locked

symptoms: Brew button is unresponsive or grayed out on display
Machine beeps but does not initiate brewing
Lights are on but no brewing action occurs
Cup size buttons do not respond to touch

fixSteps.title: Close the handle firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open and firmly close the K-Cup handle until it locks. On some models, the handle must be in a specific position for the brew button to activate.

fixSteps.title: Clean the entrance and exit needles
fixSteps.instruction: The top entrance needle pierces the K-Cup lid. Use the Keurig needle maintenance tool or a paperclip to clear clogs from both needles.

fixSteps.title: Run a water-only cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the K-Cup and try running a water-only brew to clear the system of any blockages.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 5 minutes and plug it back in. This resets the internal controller and clears temporary faults.

whenToCallPro: If the machine does not respond after needle cleaning and power cycling, an internal fault may require service.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig not brewing fix needle cleaning

faq.q: How do I clean the Keurig needles?
faq.a: Open the handle, look inside for the top needle, and use a paperclip to clear the hole. The bottom needle is in the removable K-Cup holder.

faq.q: Why does my Keurig make noise but not brew?
faq.a: The pump is running but water cannot flow, likely due to a clogged needle or air lock in the water line.

faq.q: How often should I clean the needles?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months, or whenever you notice brewing issues such as slow flow or short cups.

title: Short Cup — Incomplete Brew
shortDescription: The machine dispenses less coffee than the selected cup size.
fullDescription: A short cup means the brew cycle stopped before dispensing the full selected volume, resulting in a partial cup with weaker coffee. The most common cause is a clogged exit needle that restricts water flow through the K-Cup.

causes.cause: Clogged exit needle blocked by compacted coffee grounds
Scale buildup restricting internal water flow
Air lock in the water system
K-Cup pod too finely ground creating back-pressure
Water pump weakening from age

symptoms: Mug only partially filled after brew completes
Coffee is weaker than usual
Machine stops brewing before the expected time
Coffee grounds visible in the cup
Pumping sound stops early during the brew cycle

fixSteps.title: Clean the exit needle
fixSteps.instruction: Power off and unplug. Open the K-Cup holder. Use the Keurig needle cleaning tool or a straightened paperclip to clear the exit needle at the bottom of the pod holder.

fixSteps.title: Clean the entrance needle
fixSteps.instruction: Lift the handle. Carefully clean the top entrance needle under the lid with a paperclip. This needle punctures the top of the K-Cup and can also clog.

fixSteps.title: Run water-only flush cycles
fixSteps.instruction: With no K-Cup, run 3-4 large brew cycles to flush the water lines and push out any trapped air or loose debris.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: If short cups persist after needle cleaning, scale buildup may be restricting internal flow. Run a full descaling cycle.

whenToCallPro: If short cups persist after cleaning and descaling, the pump may be losing pressure and the machine may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig short cup half cup fix needle cleaning

faq.q: Why does my Keurig only make half a cup?
faq.a: A clogged exit needle is the most common cause. Clean both needles with the cleaning tool or a paperclip.

faq.q: Why are there coffee grounds in my cup?
faq.a: Grounds in the cup indicate a needle or seal issue. Clean the needles and ensure the K-Cup is properly seated.

faq.q: How often should I clean the needles?
faq.a: Every 1-2 weeks or whenever you notice reduced flow. The Keurig needle cleaning tool makes this quick and easy.

title: Machine Not Heating Water
shortDescription: The brewer is dispensing cold or lukewarm water instead of properly heated water.
fullDescription: When a Keurig brewer fails to heat water, it dispenses cold or lukewarm liquid during brewing. This can be caused by scale buildup insulating the heating element, a tripped thermal switch, or a failed heating element. The machine may show no specific error code.

causes.cause: Scale buildup insulating the heating element from water
Tripped thermal safety switch from an overheating event
Faulty heating element no longer producing heat
Control board failure not powering the heater circuit

symptoms: Coffee brewed is cold or lukewarm instead of hot
Machine takes an unusually long time to show the ready indicator
Ready indicator never illuminates or stays off
Steam or heat typically felt around the machine is absent
Hot cocoa and tea also come out cold

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the brewer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the Keurig and let it sit for 30 minutes. This can reset a tripped thermal switch. Plug it back in and wait for it to heat up fully.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Heavy scale on the heating element insulates it and prevents proper heat transfer to water. Run a full descaling cycle with Keurig descaling solution.

fixSteps.title: Check the power outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the outlet provides full voltage. A shared circuit under heavy load may not deliver enough power for the heating element. Try a different dedicated outlet.

fixSteps.title: Contact Keurig support
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine still does not heat after descaling and power cycling, the heating element or control board is likely faulty. Keurig machines are generally not user-serviceable for internal heating components.

whenToCallPro: If descaling and resetting do not restore heating, the machine likely needs replacement or warranty service.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig not heating water cold coffee fix

faq.q: Why is my Keurig brewing cold coffee?
faq.a: The most common cause is scale buildup on the heating element. Descale the machine first. If that does not help, the heater may be failing.

faq.q: Can I repair the heating element myself?
faq.a: Keurig machines are not designed for user repair of internal heating components. If the heater is faulty, contact Keurig support for warranty.

faq.q: How hot should Keurig water be?
faq.a: Keurig brewers heat water to approximately 192 degrees Fahrenheit (89 degrees Celsius). If noticeably cooler, the machine needs attention.

title: All Lights Flashing — System Error
shortDescription: All indicator lights or buttons on the Keurig are flashing simultaneously, indicating a general system error.
fullDescription: When all lights on a Keurig flash simultaneously, it indicates a general system error that the machine cannot self-diagnose. This catch-all error can be caused by a stuck button, internal sensor fault, or control board malfunction. It is colloquially known as the 'Christmas tree' error.

causes.cause: Stuck or shorted button on the control panel
K-Cup holder not properly seated after cleaning or pod removal
Internal blockage or air lock preventing operation
Control board fault from power surge

symptoms: All indicator lights flash simultaneously in a pattern
All buttons light up and blink together
Machine does not respond to any button presses
Machine will not brew, heat, or power off normally
Display shows no readable text

fixSteps.title: Hard reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the Keurig from the wall. Hold down the power button for 10 seconds while unplugged to discharge any residual power. Wait 5 minutes. Plug back in and power on.

fixSteps.title: Check the K-Cup holder
fixSteps.instruction: Open the lid and remove the K-Cup holder assembly. Check for stuck K-Cup remains or grounds. Clean and firmly reseat the holder until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Press each button individually
fixSteps.instruction: Sometimes a stuck button causes all lights to flash. Press each button on the panel firmly a few times to unstick it. Clean around button edges with a slightly damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Remove reservoir and reset
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water reservoir, empty it, and dry the contact points. Refill with fresh water and reseat. Try powering on again to reset the water sensor.

whenToCallPro: If a hard reset does not clear the all-lights-flashing error, the control board may be faulty. Contact Keurig for warranty replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig all lights flashing fix reset Christmas tree

faq.q: Why are all the lights on my Keurig flashing?
faq.a: This is a general system error. Try a hard reset by unplugging for 5 minutes, then check the K-Cup holder and buttons.

faq.q: Is the machine broken if all lights flash?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Most cases resolve with a hard reset. If it persists, the control board may need replacement.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause all lights to flash?
faq.a: Yes. A power surge can scramble the control board. A hard reset usually clears this.

title: K-Cup Not Recognized or Puncture Error
shortDescription: The machine cannot properly read, puncture, or brew the inserted K-Cup pod.
fullDescription: A K-Cup error occurs when the brewer cannot properly handle the K-Cup pod. The pod may not be compatible, the lid may not have been punctured by the entrance needle, the pod may be swollen from heat exposure, or the K-Cup holder is misaligned. On newer SMART models, this can also mean the pod barcode is not readable.

causes.cause: K-Cup pod not properly centered and seated in the holder
Entrance needle clogged and unable to puncture the pod lid
Swollen, expired, or damaged K-Cup pod
K-Cup holder misaligned or gasket worn

symptoms: Oops or K-Cup error message on the display
Machine will not start brewing after closing the lid
Lid does not close fully or feels misaligned
K-Cup is not punctured when you remove it
Water flows around the K-Cup instead of through it

fixSteps.title: Remove and reseat the K-Cup
fixSteps.instruction: Open the lid, remove the K-Cup. Check that it is not swollen, damaged, or the wrong size. Place a fresh K-Cup into the holder, ensuring it sits flat and centered. Close the lid firmly.

fixSteps.title: Clean the entrance needle
fixSteps.instruction: Open the lid. The entrance needle is the sharp point under the top lid. Carefully clean it with a paperclip to remove any compacted grounds blocking the needle tip.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the K-Cup holder
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the K-Cup holder assembly. Clean the exit needle at the bottom. Check that the holder is not cracked and reinsert it fully so it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Try a different K-Cup brand
fixSteps.instruction: Some third-party pods may not fit correctly in all Keurig models. Try an official Keurig K-Cup to determine if the issue is pod compatibility.

whenToCallPro: If the holder is cracked or the needle mechanism is broken, the holder assembly needs replacement. This is a user-replaceable part on most models.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig K-Cup error oops not recognized fix

faq.q: Why does my Keurig say Oops?
faq.a: The Oops message means the K-Cup was not detected or punctured properly. Remove it, clean the needle, and try again with a fresh pod.

faq.q: Can I use non-Keurig pods?
faq.a: Most third-party K-Cup compatible pods work, but some may not fit perfectly in all models. Official K-Cups are the most reliable.

faq.q: Why is my K-Cup swollen?
faq.a: K-Cups can swell from heat exposure during storage or from age. Swollen pods may not seat properly and should be discarded.

title: Machine Leaking Water
shortDescription: Water is leaking from the brewer during or between brew cycles.
fullDescription: Water leaking from a Keurig can occur from several points: the K-Cup area (damaged seal), the reservoir connection (loose seat or cracked reservoir), the drip tray (overfull), or the bottom of the machine (internal line failure).

causes.cause: Overfull drip tray that has not been emptied
Water reservoir not properly seated or cracked
Worn or damaged K-Cup holder gasket seal
Internal water line or fitting has come loose

symptoms: Water pooling under the machine on the countertop
Water dripping from the K-Cup area during brewing
Reservoir leaking where it connects to the machine
Drip tray overflowing between emptying
Water spots on countertop appearing overnight

fixSteps.title: Empty the drip tray
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and empty the drip tray. An overfull tray is the simplest and most common explanation for water around the base.

fixSteps.title: Check reservoir seating and condition
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water reservoir, inspect the bottom for hairline cracks. Check the gasket or valve where it meets the machine. Clean both contact surfaces and reseat firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the K-Cup holder seal
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the K-Cup holder. Check the rubber gasket ring around the top for tears, deformation, or coffee residue buildup. Clean or replace the gasket.

fixSteps.title: Check for internal leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Place the machine on a dry towel. Run a brew cycle and watch carefully for the leak source. If water comes from the bottom of the machine, an internal fitting has failed.

whenToCallPro: If the leak originates from inside the machine body (internal fittings), the machine typically needs replacement as Keurig internals are not user-serviceable.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig leaking water fix how to stop leak

faq.q: Why is my Keurig leaking from the bottom?
faq.a: An internal water line or fitting may have come loose. Check the drip tray first, then the reservoir seal. If the leak is from inside, the machine may need replacement.

faq.q: Can a cracked reservoir cause leaking?
faq.a: Yes. Inspect the reservoir carefully for hairline cracks. Replacement reservoirs are available and inexpensive.

faq.q: Why does water leak around the K-Cup?
faq.a: The seal gasket around the K-Cup holder may be worn or have coffee residue preventing a proper seal. Clean or replace the gasket.

title: Lid or Handle Not Fully Closed
shortDescription: The brew head lid or handle is not fully closed, preventing the brew cycle from starting.
fullDescription: The machine detects the lid or handle position with a magnetic switch or mechanical sensor. If not fully closed, the machine will not brew as a safety measure to prevent hot water from spraying during the brew cycle.

causes.cause: Lid or handle not pushed down firmly enough to latch
K-Cup sitting too high in the holder preventing closure
Coffee ground debris preventing full lid closure
Faulty lid position switch or magnet dislodged

symptoms: Lid or handle warning on display or blinking indicator
Machine will not brew despite lid appearing closed
Lid feels springy and does not latch firmly
Brew button is unresponsive or grayed out

fixSteps.title: Close the lid firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Push the lid or handle down firmly until you hear or feel it click into the fully closed position. Apply even downward pressure.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the lid. Look for old K-Cup fragments, coffee grounds, or debris around the lid hinge and closure mechanism. Clean thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the K-Cup holder
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinsert the K-Cup holder. If it was misaligned, it could prevent the lid from closing fully against the gasket.

fixSteps.title: Check the lid magnet
fixSteps.instruction: Some models use a magnetic reed switch to detect lid position. If the small magnet in the lid has cracked or fallen out, the switch will not activate and the lid is not detected as closed.

whenToCallPro: If the lid magnet or position switch is faulty, contact Keurig support for warranty replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig lid not closing handle error fix

faq.q: Why won't my Keurig detect the lid is closed?
faq.a: Most likely debris is preventing full closure. Open the lid, clean the area around the hinge and lock, and close firmly until it clicks.

faq.q: Can a K-Cup prevent the lid from closing?
faq.a: If a K-Cup is sitting too high in the holder due to a swollen pod or misaligned holder, the lid cannot close fully.

faq.q: Is there a magnet in the Keurig lid?
faq.a: Yes, many models use a small magnet in the lid and a reed switch in the body to detect closure. If the magnet falls out, the lid is not detected.

title: Machine Not Dispensing Any Water
shortDescription: The machine runs but no water or coffee is dispensed from the spout at all.
fullDescription: When the Keurig sounds like it is working (pump runs, heating activates) but absolutely no liquid comes out, there is a complete blockage in the water path. This is distinct from a short cup — no water at all is dispensed. Causes include a severe air lock, completely clogged needles, or pump failure.

causes.cause: Complete air lock in the water system
Both entrance and exit needles completely clogged
Severe scale buildup blocking all internal water passages
Water pump failure — motor runs but impeller broken

symptoms: Machine powers on and heats up normally
Pump sounds run but absolutely nothing comes out
Empty mug after brew cycle appears to complete
Machine may show PRIME error afterward
Gurgling, sputtering, or loud pump noises

fixSteps.title: Break the air lock
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the reservoir. Turn the Keurig upside down over a sink and shake gently several times. Turn right side up. Fill the reservoir and reseat. Try a brew cycle with no K-Cup.

fixSteps.title: Slap the bottom of the machine
fixSteps.instruction: With the machine unplugged, turn it upside down and firmly slap the bottom several times. This can dislodge trapped air and debris in the water line. Return to upright position.

fixSteps.title: Clean all needles thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Clean both the entrance and exit needles with a paperclip, removing all compacted grounds. Also remove and rinse the K-Cup holder assembly under running water.

fixSteps.title: Soak with concentrated vinegar
fixSteps.instruction: If the above steps do not work, fill the reservoir with undiluted white vinegar and let it soak for 4 hours. Then run brew cycles to flush the vinegar through. Follow with 12+ fresh water rinse cycles.

whenToCallPro: If absolutely nothing dispenses after all cleaning and descaling attempts, the pump has likely failed. Keurig machines are generally replaced rather than repaired for pump failures.
videoSearchQuery: Keurig not dispensing no water coming out fix

faq.q: Why does my Keurig run but nothing comes out?
faq.a: Most commonly an air lock or complete clog. Try turning the machine upside down briefly, then clean all needles and descale.

faq.q: How do I break an air lock in a Keurig?
faq.a: Remove the reservoir, shake or turn the machine upside down, reseat the reservoir, and run blank brew cycles to force water through.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing if the pump died?
faq.a: Keurig pump repairs are generally not cost-effective. If under warranty, contact Keurig for replacement. Otherwise a new machine may be more practical.
